_meta.purpose: Per-state anchor_as_of: the date of the 3rd-party (NerdWallet/MoneyGeek) snapshot each state's current calibration is anchored to. Load-bearing for drift: apply_filed_changes.py only drifts filings whose effective date is AFTER a state's anchor_as_of — earlier filings are already embedded in that snapshot, so applying them would double-count. RESET drift to 1 (i.e. bump anchor_as_of forward) whenever a state is recalibrated to a fresh snapshot.
_meta.default_source: cheapest_by_state.json snapshot — NerdWallet + MoneyGeek, 2026-06-23 (the 2026-06-23 full model rebuild, calibrate_model.py)
_meta.how_to_update: When you re-anchor a state to a newer NerdWallet/MoneyGeek pull, set that state's anchor_as_of to the new snapshot date. Leave others on the global default.
_meta.date_basis: Compared against each filing's effective_new (new business sees the filed rate first); fall back to effective_renewal when effective_new is null.
